QF.id: VB Legal Structure of Mutual Funds_19

question.text: Mr. Das, a retired government teacher, has passed the NISM Series V-B exam but has not yet registered with AMFI for his ARN. Can he start selling mutual fund schemes to his friends and neighbours?

answer.text: No — he must first register with AMFI and obtain his ARN before selling any mutual fund
Yes — passing the NISM exam is sufficient to start selling immediately
Yes — but only if he sells to close family members
Yes — he can sell for 30 days without ARN as a grace period

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Passing the NISM exam is necessary but NOT sufficient. After passing, the person must register with AMFI and obtain an ARN (AMFI Registration Number). Only after receiving the ARN can they legally distribute mutual funds and receive commissions. There is no grace period or family exception. **The Trap:** There is no provision for selling without an ARN, even temporarily. The NISM certificate alone does not authorize distribution — the ARN does. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under distributor compliance. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** After passing your exam, do not delay your AMFI registration. The sooner you get your ARN, the sooner you can start your distribution practice. Gather your documents (NISM certificate, PAN, photographs, KYC) and apply to AMFI immediately.
